name: Old Fashioned Popcorn Balls
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Karo light or dark corn syrup
1 cup Firmly packed dark brown sugar
recipeInstructions:
Servings: 10
Place the popped corn in a large greased bowl or pan. In a heavy saucepan (2 quarts), stir together corn syrup, sugar, water, and vinegar. Stirring constantly, cook over medium heat until the mixture boils. Continue cooking, stirring occasionally, until a small amount of mixture dropped into very cold water forms a ball hard enough to hold its shape but still soft enough to plastically deform. Remove from heat; stir in margarine. Slowly pour over popcorn, stirring to coat well.
When cool enough to handle yet still quite warm, quickly shape into balls with hands well-buttered or oiled. Wrap individually in plastic wrap. Makes about 10 3-inch balls.
Option  Substitute 2 tablespoons of praline liqueur for 2 tablespoons of water.
